description: In 2018, Taiwan held a series of referendums, including Measure 12, which proposed defining marriage as between a man and a woman in the Civil Code, effectively challenging the 2017 Constitutional Court ruling that mandated marriage equality. This scenario tracks the public opinion shift regarding traditional marriage definitions vs. LGBTQ+ equality leading up to the November 2018 vote.

key_events.description: Coalition for the Happiness of Our Next Generation gathers over 500,000 signatures to initiate anti-same-sex marriage referendums.
Conservative religious groups launch high-visibility media campaigns framing traditional family structures as essential to social stability.
Public debate intensifies following a high-profile televised session on the impact of marriage equality on education and traditional values.
Referendum results pass, with voters overwhelmingly supporting the definition of marriage as between a man and a woman.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring the gap between judicial/elite-driven social progress and grassroots conservative backlash in a democratic Asian context.
